description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent cross-gabled roof clad in a muted brown material. The exterior walls are finished in a dark, brick-like texture, and the house sits on a rectangular green lawn. The front facade includes a covered porch area with a light-colored floor and a main entrance door, flanked by a set of three display boards or signs. Windows are symmetrically placed on the front and side elevations, with white frames that contrast against the dark wall finish. The rear and side views reveal additional windows and the continuation of the brick pattern, with the roof extending to provide ample overhangs. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled brown roof, dark brick-textured walls, white-framed windows, and a covered front porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, three display boards, and a large window to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cross-gabled roof, a single window, and the extension of the porch area.
description_view_2: Rear view with two symmetrically placed windows and the continuation of the dark brick wall texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a solid wall with no windows and the full span of the roof overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with additional side and rear windows
